abstract: To address the persistent challenge of metal contamination in traditional atom transfer radical polymerization (ATRP), we report a metal-free ATRP process mediated by light and driven by an organic photoredox catalyst. This approach enables the efficient activation and deactivation of vinyl monomer polymerization via light, providing precise control over molecular weight, polydispersity, and chain-end fidelity. Notably, the system facilitates the straightforward synthesis of block copolymers and integrates seamlessly with other controlled radical methodologies, offering significant structural and synthetic versatility. These organic photoredox catalysts represent a powerful alternative to traditional metal-based systems, opening new avenues for applications in materials science and providing a robust toolset for both small molecule and polymer synthesis.
